skill.name: android-jetpack-compose-expert
skill.description: Expert guidance for building modern Android UIs with Jetpack Compose, including state management, navigation, performance, and Material Design 3.
skill.summary: Build production-quality Android screens with Jetpack Compose patterns for state, navigation, and performance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance indicators. AI review dismissed these as false positives from Markdown backticks, Compose examples, and Android UI terminology. No executable scripts, network access, secret handling, prompt injection text, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command findings occur in Markdown inline code or fenced Kotlin and TOML examples. These lines document Jetpack Compose patterns and do not instruct the assistant to run shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation with Kotlin and TOML examples. No shell command, process execution API, or instruction to execute local commands appears at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections do not correspond to cryptographic APIs or algorithms. The cited lines contain skill metadata, dependency guidance, navigation comments, and NavHost setup text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain Android UI guidance and no hashing, encryption, TLS, or password-storage logic. The static signal is not semantically related to cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections appear in descriptive Markdown and Compose sample code. The skill does not request host enumeration, environment inspection, file discovery, or network probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is Android Compose documentation. No evidence found of commands or instructions that collect system, account, repository, or network information.

content.value_statement: Jetpack Compose projects often struggle with state, navigation, and recomposition behavior. This skill gives Claude, Codex, or Claude Code a focused Android UI playbook.
actual_capabilities: Guides project dependency setup for Jetpack Compose and Material Design 3.
Explains ViewModel and StateFlow patterns for predictable UI state.
Shows screen and content composable separation for reusable UI components.
Describes type-safe Navigation Compose setup with serializable destinations.
Provides recomposition guidance using remember, derivedStateOf, and stability annotations.
limitations: It is a documentation skill, not a runnable Android project template.
It does not include automated tests, Gradle files, or generated source files.
Dependency versions may need updates for newer Android Gradle Plugin releases.
It gives general guidance and cannot validate app-specific architecture alone.

use_cases.title: Start a Compose Screen
use_cases.description: Plan a new screen with state, loading, error, and content states before implementation.

use_cases.title: Migrate XML Layouts
use_cases.description: Convert legacy Android UI flows into Compose components with clear state ownership.

use_cases.title: Review Compose Performance
use_cases.description: Identify recomposition risks and improve stability for lists, callbacks, and derived values.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Screen Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new feature screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline a Jetpack Compose screen for [feature]. Include UI state, events, loading, error, and success behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor State Ownership
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate developer improving an existing screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Compose screen for state ownership issues. Suggest a ViewModel and StateFlow structure without changing the user experience.

prompt_templates.title: Design Type-Safe Navigation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced developer adding navigation between screens.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design type-safe Navigation Compose routes for these destinations: [destinations]. Include arguments and back-stack concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Recomposition Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Compose UI for recomposition and stability risks. Focus on remember, derivedStateOf, callbacks, list state, and expensive work.

output_examples.input: Ask for a new profile screen architecture.
output_examples.output: A screen plan with UI state fields, ViewModel events, content composables, and error handling responsibilities.

output_examples.input: Ask for navigation guidance for home and profile screens.
output_examples.output: A type-safe route design with destination responsibilities, argument handling, and where each screen should trigger navigation.

output_examples.input: Ask for performance review of a slow Compose list.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review of recomposition triggers, unstable parameters, expensive calculations, and state placement improvements.

best_practices: Keep screen state immutable and expose it from ViewModels as read-only StateFlow.
Separate stateful screen composables from stateless content composables.
Use remember and derivedStateOf for expensive values that depend on Compose state.
anti_patterns: Do not expose MutableStateFlow directly from a ViewModel.
Do not pass ViewModel instances through deeply nested composables.
Do not run expensive sorting or mapping directly in a composable body.

faq.question: Does this skill create Android project files?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples that an assistant can apply to your existing project.

faq.question: Can it help with Material Design 3?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes Material Design 3 setup and Compose UI guidance.

faq.question: Does it cover ViewModel state management?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends StateFlow, immutable UI state, and clear event handling.

faq.question: Can it review recomposition problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It highlights remember, derivedStateOf, stable models, and expensive composable work.

faq.question: Is it tied to one app architecture?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on common MVVM and MVI patterns used in Compose applications.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only documentation content and dismissed the static security alerts as false positives.
